sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions after second RIGHT
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Board region (rows 25-38, cols 8-55):")
for r in range(25, 39):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r:3d}: {ascii_lines[r][8:56]}")
# Check segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nGreen squares:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, bounds: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Left green square (id=11): rows 33-35, cols 18-20 (moved from cols 15-17 to cols 18-20)
- Right green square (id=6): rows 30-32, cols 27-29 (still at same position!)
So the pattern is clear: when the two green squares are at different rows, only the one at the LOWER row (left green at rows 33-35) moves horizontally. The one at the higher row (right green at rows 30-32) doesn't move.
But wait, I need to understand the movement rules better. Let me think about this:
- When both green squares are at the same row, horizontal movement affects both
- When they're at different rows, horizontal movement only affects the lower one
Or maybe the rule is simpler: horizontal movement always affects the "active" green square, which is determined by... something.
Actually, looking more carefully:
- Left green square has white center (W) - id=11 with children=[12]
- Right green square has black center (B) - id=6 with children=[8]
The left green square is the one that responds to UP/DOWN. When they're at different rows, LEFT/RIGHT only moves the one that was last moved vertically?
Let me think about this differently. The key observation is:
1. UP/DOWN moves the left green square (the one with white center)
2. When both are at the same row, LEFT/RIGHT moves both
3. When they're at different rows, LEFT/RIGHT only moves the left green square
So the rule might be: LEFT/RIGHT moves all green squares that are at the same row as the "active" one, and the active one is determined by the last vertical movement.
OK, let me now think about the strategy. I need to:
1. Get the left green square back into the left box
2. Get the right green square into the right box
The left green square is at rows 33-35, cols 18-20. The left box is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. So I need to move LEFT by 3 and the green square would be at cols 15-17, still not inside the box. I need to move LEFT by 6 to get to cols 12-14, which is inside the box.
But wait, the left green square is at rows 33-35, and the left box interior is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14. So if I move LEFT twice (6 cells), the green square would be at cols 12-14, which matches the box interior.
But the right green square is at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Since they're at different rows, LEFT should only move the left green square.
Let me try LEFT, LEFT to get the left green square back into the box.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left green square is at rows 33-35, cols 18-20
# Left box interior is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14
# Need to move LEFT by 6 (2x LEFT) to get back inside the box
# Right green square is at a different row, so it shouldn't move
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
